title: Agents, Workflows, and the Control Loop · Operations Recall
summary: Compare a fixed workflow with an adaptive tool-using agent. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an agent-system boundary map after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish predefined workflows from model-directed agents and choose the simpler sufficient architecture.
Model the observe, decide, act, and verify cycle with explicit state transitions.
Separate model context, application state, external state, and durable records.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Workflow Agent evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Workflow Agent assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Distinguish predefined workflows from model-directed agents and choose the simpler sufficient architecture. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the boundaries of the idea, compare a valid case with a near miss, and trace the causal difference.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: loops foundations. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Workflow Agent, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an agent-system boundary map.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Every multi-step automation requires an autonomous agent. This fails the Workflow Agent contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

lines.label: assumption

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Loop Invariants applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State invariants that must hold before and after every loop iteration.
